summary: The recent arXiv window contains no strong direct heavy-ion collision submission after abstract-level screening. The scientifically strongest item is a HEP-specific symbolic-regression workflow for discovering analytic background functions, while the most transferable physics-AI papers concern conservative learned finite-volume solvers, label-free finite-volume-residual training on meshes, and uncertainty-aware physics-augmented constitutive modeling. The recurring themes are symbolic regression for analysis models, physically constrained surrogate solvers, uncertainty propagation, mesh-native graph networks, and measured robustness rather than generic ML novelty.
strongest_paper: 2607.19750: symbolic regression plus uncertainty modeling for HEP background-function discovery, demonstrated on CMS and ATLAS Run 2 dijet spectra.

papers.title: Machine Can Automatically Discover Parametric Functions to Model HEP Data
papers.authors_short: Ho Fung Tsoi, Dylan Rankin, Cecile Caillol, Miles Cranmer, Sridhara Dasu, Javier Duarte, Philip Harris, Elliot Lipeles
papers.abstract: The paper introduces SymbolFit, pairing symbolic regression with uncertainty modeling to automate the search for analytic functions used to model binned HEP spectra. On CMS and ATLAS Run 2 dijet spectra, 560 seeded runs across seven fit configurations generated more than 1000 viable functions with chi2/NDF near 1, and 111 runs rediscovered the published dijet and UA2 forms.

papers.title: Hard Guarantees at a Measured Price: Entropy-Stable Learned Finite Volumes for Compressible Flow
papers.abstract: The paper develops an admissibility-preserving, entropy-stable learned finite-volume scheme for two-dimensional Euler flow on unstructured meshes. It emphasizes pre-fixed evaluation protocols, negative controls, iso-cost comparisons, and factor decomposition; the guaranteed variant completed 36 of 36 rollouts with zero negativity events, while learned components helped mainly in selected wall-boundary cases.

papers.title: Label-Free Finite-Volume-Residual Training of Attention Graph Neural Networks for Coupled Thermo-Fluid Fields
papers.authors_short: Tianyu Li, Zhiwei Cao, Qingang Zhang, Ruihang Wang, Binyang Song, Yonggang Wen
papers.abstract: The authors train an attention graph neural network directly against finite-volume residuals of the governing equations, evaluated on the mesh, avoiding labeled CFD solution data. They report 2.3-2.8% all-field nRMSE on steady benchmarks, close buoyancy-energy coupling behavior, and stronger accuracy than a supervised baseline on two transient parametric cases without paying the data-generation cost.

papers.title: Interval and fuzzy physics-augmented neural networks (iPANN and fPANN) for uncertainty quantification and propagation in constitutive modeling
papers.authors_short: Somesh Pratap Singh, Govinda Anantha Padmanabha, Jingye Tan, Steven Yang, Reese E. Jones, D. Thomas Seidl, Nikolaos Bouklas
papers.abstract: This work builds interval and fuzzy physics-augmented neural networks for uncertainty-aware hyperelastic constitutive modeling under sparse, noisy, or heterogeneous stress-deformation data. The models enforce objectivity, consistency, and polyconvexity-promoting structure, then propagate learned lower/mean/upper branches through finite-element simulations.

cautions.title: PG-KINN: A Physics-Informed Petrov-Galerkin Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Solving Forward and Inverse PDEs
cautions.authors_short: Amirhossein Sadr, Nima Soltani, Vahideh Moghtadaiee, Aida Pakniyat, Dara Rahmati, Saeid Gorgin
cautions.abstract: The paper proposes a Petrov-Galerkin KAN formulation for forward and inverse PDE problems and reports broad benchmark wins across mechanics examples.
